title: acer hyrcanum subsp. keckianum
intro: Acer hyrcanum subsp. keckianum, also known as the Keck's Persian maple, is a subspecies of maple tree native to the Caucasus region and Iran. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids in its bark and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and fever, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Keck's Persian maple, such as tea or tincture, can be made by infusing the dried bark and leaves in hot water or a solvent, and have been used to support overall health and well-being.
